threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still queue for the bus like it's a normal thing—locals know it's every person for themselves.
Last night on the 333, saw a guy serenade a dog with a guitar, and the mutt was howling along like it was a legendary concert. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a neighbor using an old washing machine as a plant stand, I realized Neukölln is all about repurposing and creativity.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, you can find a bar in an old subway station, complete with platform seating and graffiti art, totally unique vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know a local by their disdain for those 'safety' announcements about the Civic Center BART stop. It's just part of the daily drama.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the bus crowd with a ukulele at Civic Center—Muni delays or not, he had everyone smiling. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every local café is a ‘postcard-perfect’ spot, but the real gems are often the ones with mismatched chairs and a distant hum of construction.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still stare at the metro maps like they're puzzles, while locals glide through without a second thought.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the ‘bus stop’ is just a pause, not a way of life. Locals treat it like a second home—conversations, coffee, the whole nine yards.
Caught a glimpse of a guy on the N155 convincing a girl the moon was following them. She was giggling, but he looked dead serious.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last week, I saw a toddler casually riding a unicycle past a protest for better public transport. Only in Neukölln, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice the spontaneous dance parties on the S-Bahn? One minute it's quiet; next, it's a flash mob of locals just vibing. So Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows that BART's 'loud bang' is just the train's way of saying, 'Welcome back, hope you enjoy the smoke!'

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo jump onto a bus, still holding a half-eaten burrito. Only in the TL would this feel completely normal.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just waltz into the local markets. But you haven't truly shopped until you've bargained over ripe tomatoes with a señora.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their frustration at the tram schedule—most locals know it’s just a suggestion after 8 PM.
